rulings.Case Title: ROSA MARTINEZ VS. AIRBNB, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Airbnb Defendants' Responses To Interrogatories; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Jose Becerra In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY CALENDAR FOR FRIDAY, JUN-27-2025, LINE 1. 2-PLAINTIFF ROSA MARTINEZ', INDIVIDUALLY AND AS PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OF THE ESTATE OF SEBASTIAN MEJIA. Motion To Compel Airbnb Defendants' Responses To Interrogatories. Continued to July 11, 2025 to be heard by Judge Van Aken per CCP Section 170.6 challenge. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JT)

rulings.Case Title: ROSA MARTINEZ VS. AIRBNB, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Airbnb Defendants' Responses To Request For Production Of Documents; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Jose Becerra In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY CALENDAR FOR FRIDAY, JUN-27-2025, LINE 1. 1-PLAINTIFF ROSA MARTINEZ', INDIVIDUALLY AND AS PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OF THE ESTATE OF SEBASTIAN MEJIA, Motion To Compel Airbnb Defendants' Responses To Request For Production Of Documents. Continued to July 11, 2025 to be heard by Judge Van Aken per CCP Section 170.6 challenge. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JT)

rulings.Case Title: BHAGBAT KHANAL ET AL VS. RI LONG LIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY CALENDAR FOR FRIDAY, JUN-27-2025, LINE 3. DEFENDANTS RI LIN, EAN HOLDINGS, LLC, and ENTERPRISE RENT-A-CAR OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Granted as unopposed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JT)

rulings.Case Title: THERESA BAYLISS-PEDROTTI VS. CALTRANS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY CALENDAR FOR FRIDAY, JUN-27-2025, LINE 8. DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S, ACTING BY AND THROUGH THE DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION SUED HERIN AS CALTRANS DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant California Highway Patrol's ("CHP") demurrer to the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is overruled. ***THE FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO THE PARTIES*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JT)
